title: flag registry record — records/flags/FLAG-NNNN.json
description: One feature flag, one owned file (patterned on baseline's per-claim records). The FLAG family is opt-in: it activates when the descriptor sets uses_feature_flags:true or records/flags/ is non-empty. This schema binds shape; FLAG-02 polices the non-empty owner and FLAG-03 the review_by freshness (present unless type ∈ {kill-switch, permission}, which carry long review horizons). The toggle-metadata field set originates in Fowler's Feature Toggles article; default review horizons are informed by Unleash's shipped per-type lifetimes.

id.description: The record's identity — must match the filename.

name.description: The flag key as it appears in code / the flag store (e.g. 'new-checkout-flow').

owner.description: Who owns this flag — the answer to 'who can turn this off?'. FLAG-02 requires this be non-empty.

type.description: Flag species (Unleash's type table). kill-switch/permission are permanent-class: FLAG-03 exempts them from near-term expiry, requiring review_by within permanent_flag_review_days instead.

created.description: When the flag was introduced. Anchors the carrying-cost clock.

review_by.description: Expiry / review date. FLAG-03 requires this present (unless a permanent type) and not past-due. Default horizons: release 40d · experiment 40d · operational 7d · sunset 90d · kill-switch/permission permanent-class.
